reflection.theme: The Middle of the Year, the Middle of the Work
reflection.quote: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
reflection.reading:
Today is the last day of June. Half the year is behind you. If you're sober right now, that counts — not because anyone's keeping score, but because you know what it took to get here. The mornings you didn't want. The Friday nights you stayed in, or went out and ordered something with no proof in it. The phone calls you made instead of the ones you would have made at 2 a.m. eleven years ago.

The middle of anything is the unglamorous part. The first day of sobriety has a story. The thousandth day is just a Tuesday. But the Tuesdays are where the life gets built. Durant's line about Aristotle gets quoted to death, but it's true: you don't become a sober person by deciding to be one. You become one by doing the small repeated things — going to bed, returning the text, eating before you're starving, telling someone the truth when the lie would be easier.

Don't wait for July to mean something. Don't wait for a new month, a new chip, a new feeling. The work is in what you do today, in the ordinary hours, when nobody is watching and nothing is on fire. That's where the actual life lives. That's where you live now.
reflection.meditation: What's one small, ordinary thing I can do well today — not because it's exciting, but because it's mine to do?

affirmations: I am building my life in the ordinary hours, not waiting for a better day to start.
Today I have agency over how I respond, even when I don't have control over what happens.
I am someone who can be counted on — starting with the promises I make to myself.

gratitude.prayer: I'm grateful for a quiet Tuesday and a clear head to meet it with. I'm grateful for the version of me that kept going through the months I didn't feel like it — because he's the reason I get this one. For coffee that actually tastes like something, for the people who text back, for a body that wakes up without dread.
gratitude.closing: Halfway through the year, still here. That's enough for today.
